Stub functions for initializing, managing, and controlling a KinoLayer in the Pebble firmware.

# Purpose
The code defines a set of functions for managing and interacting with a `KinoLayer` object, which is part of a user interface library. The primary purpose of these functions is to create, configure, and control a `KinoLayer`, which appears to be a graphical component that can display and manipulate visual content, referred to as a "reel." The functions include initialization and deinitialization ([`kino_layer_init`](<#kino_layer_init>), [`kino_layer_deinit`](<#kino_layer_deinit>)), creation and destruction ([`kino_layer_create`](<#kino_layer_create>), [`kino_layer_destroy`](<#kino_layer_destroy>)), and various configuration options such as setting the reel ([`kino_layer_set_reel`](<#kino_layer_set_reel>), [`kino_layer_set_reel_with_resource`](<#kino_layer_set_reel_with_resource>), [`kino_layer_set_reel_with_resource_system`](<#kino_layer_set_reel_with_resource_system>)), background color ([`kino_layer_set_background_color`](<#kino_layer_set_background_color>)), and alignment ([`kino_layer_set_alignment`](<#kino_layer_set_alignment>)).

The code also provides functions to control playback of the content within the `KinoLayer`, such as playing ([`kino_layer_play`](<#kino_layer_play>)), playing a specific section ([`kino_layer_play_section`](<#kino_layer_play_section>)), pausing ([`kino_layer_pause`](<#kino_layer_pause>)), and rewinding ([`kino_layer_rewind`](<#kino_layer_rewind>)). Additionally, it includes functions to retrieve properties of the `KinoLayer`, such as the underlying `Layer` object ([`kino_layer_get_layer`](<#kino_layer_get_layer>)), the current reel ([`kino_layer_get_reel`](<#kino_layer_get_reel>)), the player ([`kino_layer_get_player`](<#kino_layer_get_player>)), background color ([`kino_layer_get_background_color`](<#kino_layer_get_background_color>)), alignment ([`kino_layer_get_alignment`](<#kino_layer_get_alignment>)), and reel bounds ([`kino_layer_get_reel_bounds`](<#kino_layer_get_reel_bounds>)). The code also allows setting callbacks for the `KinoLayer` through [`kino_layer_set_callbacks`](<#kino_layer_set_callbacks>), enabling custom behavior during its lifecycle. This collection of functions provides a comprehensive interface for managing the visual and interactive aspects of a `KinoLayer` within an application.
# Imports and Dependencies

---
- `applib/ui/kino/kino_layer.h`
